issues.title: D2's cited 'existing reset-layout control' does not exist — no way to undo a resize/drag back to the computed layout
issues.description: plan.md's Approach section justifies NOT re-running dagre after a resize (D2) with: "The existing reset-layout control returns to the computed layout." No such control exists. The only 'reset' affordance anywhere in the codebase is `resetView()` (src/render/dom/runtime.ts:811-816), which only resets pan/zoom (`scale=1; tx=0; ty=0`) — it never touches `positions`/`sizes`, so it has zero effect on a node the user dragged or resized. Confirmed via source (grep for 'reset' across src/ finds nothing else) and by driving the real toolbar in a browser: it has exactly five buttons — fit (⤢), zoom in (+), zoom out (−), Save SVG, Save PNG — no reset-layout button, no keyboard shortcut, no double-click gesture. `RuntimeHandle` (the public API a host app could call) likewise exposes no method to restore a node's original dagre-computed position/size; `importLayout()` only helps if the caller separately saved the original `exportLayout()` snapshot before editing, which the runtime does not do automatically (though it does keep an internal `baseSizes`, that's not exposed). This is not a new regression — dragging a node has had the same 'no undo' gap since v2 — but v0.3.0's own plan explicitly cites a mitigating control as the reason it's safe to skip re-layout on resize, and that citation is false, so the plan's risk acceptance for D2 rests on an incorrect premise.
issues.proposed_solution: NEEDS-USER-DECISION (two valid paths, a scope choice, not a bug fix): (a) AGENT-FIXABLE — add a real 'reset layout' toolbar control (and/or a RuntimeHandle.resetLayout() method) that restores every node's position AND size to the values layout() computed (the runtime already tracks the pre-edit sizes internally as `baseSizes`; positions would need the same treatment), and clears the persisted localStorage entry — closing the gap D2 assumed was already closed. (b) Accept the gap for v0.3.0 and correct plan.md's D2 text so it stops citing a control that doesn't exist (document the limitation instead: resizing/dragging is a one-way manual edit today; recovery is clearing the page's localStorage or reloading a saved layout.json). Either is reasonable; which one depends on how much the user cares about an in-UI undo for the standalone HTML export (no dev-console access) versus keeping v0.3.0 scoped to FR1-FR5 as accepted.
issues.fix_summary: D5=A: added a real Reset layout (⟲) toolbar button + RuntimeHandle.resetLayout() in src/render/dom/runtime.ts — restores every node's computed position AND size (from baseSizes + the shipped model), re-routes all edges (computePorts re-spreads the perimeter anchors), and clears the persisted localStorage layout so a reload stays reset; pan/zoom is left untouched (resetView stays separate). Ships in the standalone HTML export (serialized runtime) and via DeferredHandle in src/render/dom/index.ts. Makes plan.md D2's 'reset-layout control' real. Covered by a new unit test (dom-runtime-parity: drag+resize→resetLayout→computed positions/sizes, edges re-routed, localStorage cleared, pan/zoom kept) and a new e2e (interactive-editing.spec.ts: drag+resize→click Reset→restored→reload stays reset). README interactive section documents the button.

issues.title: Edge-handle (.vnm-edge-handle) visibility does not track selection in a real browser — a plain select never reveals it, and deselecting never hides it (reopens REV-006's residual)
issues.description: REV-006 (round-01 review) flagged that FR7's edge-handle interaction had zero pointer-event coverage and handed real-browser verification to phase ④, warning explicitly that a hit-test/capture defect found here must reopen the concern. Driving the ACTUAL shipped standalone HTML in a real Chromium (not the fake-DOM unit harness) surfaces exactly that: (1) selecting a node via a genuine pointerdown+pointerup click (no movement) correctly runs selectNode() — the blue selection outline appears immediately — but its incident .vnm-edge-handle elements stay display:none; they only become visible after some OTHER action calls renderEdges() (e.g. dragging the node even 1px, resizing it, clicking Reset, or importLayout()). Root cause: selectNode() (src/render/dom/runtime.ts, near line 1251) calls only positionHandles() (the resize-corner squares) — it never calls positionEdgeHandles()/renderEdges(). (2) The mirror bug: deselect() (same file) calls only hideHandles() (resize squares) — never positionEdgeHandles() — so once an edge handle IS visible, clicking away (a background pan, which calls deselect() at pointerdown) never hides it; it lingers floating on screen indefinitely through subsequent pans/zooms, since panning alone never calls renderEdges() either. Confirmed precisely via a diagnostic script reading the handle's live style.display: 'none' right after a plain select-click (though the outline confirms selected=A already), then 'block' after a trivial nudge-drag; and still 'block' after clicking empty canvas to deselect, and still 'block' after a subsequent pan. Screenshots confirm both symptoms visually: a floating blue dot from a prior pin remains on screen after the owning node is deselected. This is exactly the class of hit-testing/rendering-layering defect a fake DOM cannot catch — the unit-level regression test (test/interactive-subgraph-drag.test.ts) papers over it by calling a no-op handle.importLayout({...}) immediately after its simulated click 'to force a render pass', which is precisely the missing call in production selectNode(). Impact: FR7's headline discovery flow (select a node -> see grab handles on its edges -> drag one) does not work as documented/reviewed; a real user selecting a node sees nothing and has no way to know handles exist unless they first do an unrelated drag/resize. The stale-handle side effect is also a latent mis-click risk: a floating handle from a previously-selected, now-deselected node remains hit-testable at its last position and could be grabbed by accident.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE, small and well-scoped: mirror the existing resize-handle pattern — selectNode() should also call positionEdgeHandles(computePorts()) (or simply renderEdges(), which already does this plus the path/label recompute) right after positionHandles(), and deselect() should do the same right after hideHandles(), so edge-handle visibility is refreshed synchronously with every selection change, not only as an incidental side effect of some other render-triggering action. Regression coverage: e2e/interactive-editing.spec.ts now carries two dedicated repro tests under the 'TEST-002' describe block, each marked test.fail() (documenting the current defect without turning the CI-visible suite red) — 'a plain click selects the node but does NOT reveal its edge handles' and 'deselecting does not hide an already-shown handle'. Once selectNode()/deselect() are fixed to call positionEdgeHandles()/renderEdges(), BOTH of these tests will start passing, which Playwright will flag as an unexpected pass (test.fail() tests that stop failing are reported as a run failure) — that flip is the exact regression signal implement/review should look for; remove the test.fail() annotations (and delete the two now-redundant repro tests, or repurpose them as ordinary positive assertions) once fixed. All other FR7 mechanics (pin math, persistence, reset-clears, pin-then-resize re-clamp) are unaffected by this bug and pass cleanly once the workaround (a tiny nudge-drag after select) is used to make the handles visible for those tests.
issues.fix_summary: Mirrored the resize-handle pattern for edge-endpoint handles in src/render/dom/runtime.ts: selectNode() now calls positionEdgeHandles(computePorts()) right after positionHandles(), and deselect() calls it right after hideHandles(). Edge-handle visibility now tracks selection synchronously — a plain select-click reveals the selected node's incident .vnm-edge-handle elements immediately (no intervening drag/resize/import), and deselecting hides them at once (no stale floating dot). Flows into the standalone HTML export via the .toString()-serialized runtime. Removed the two test.fail() annotations in e2e/interactive-editing.spec.ts (defect repros → normal passing regression tests: select-reveals-handles + deselect-hides-handles) and refreshed the stale describe/comment text. Tightened the FR7 fake-DOM unit test in test/interactive-subgraph-drag.test.ts by deleting the masking no-op importLayout() call so it now asserts the SOURCE handle is display:block right after the select-click with NO intervening layout call; added a dedicated unit test asserting select-shows / deselect-hides with no intervening render — so the unit suite would also have caught this. Green: build + typecheck + 301 unit (300 baseline + 1 new) + 62 e2e (both de-annotated tests passing normally).
